_comment: SPRITE FORMAT ALIGNMENT contract for char_<id>_sheet.png (player + 8 NPCs ONLY -- see enemy_sheets below for the separate, unrelated enemy format). This is the STARDEW-FARMER-STYLE extended format the user authors real art against; the generator (tools/gen_placeholders.gd) and slicer (scripts/util/sprite_sheets.gd) both read this exact layout. Swap a hand-drawn char_<id>_sheet.png in at these exact dimensions/rows and it drops in with ZERO code changes.
frame_note: Each cell is frame_w x frame_h (16x32), tightly packed with NO spacing/padding between cells, transparent background, feet at the BOTTOM edge of each cell. 4 columns per row (sheet is 64px wide). 8 rows (sheet is 256px tall), 32 frames total.

row_map.frame_note: col0 = idle/standing pose, cols 1-3 = walk cycle
col0 = idle/standing pose, cols 1-3 = walk cycle
col0 = idle/standing pose, cols 1-3 = walk cycle
col0 = idle/standing pose, cols 1-3 = walk cycle; LEFT IS ITS OWN DRAWN ROW, see left_is_drawn_not_mirrored
shared by tool-use (hoe/water/harvest) AND sword swing -- see action_row_is_shared
shared by tool-use (hoe/water/harvest) AND sword swing -- see action_row_is_shared
shared by tool-use (hoe/water/harvest) AND sword swing -- see action_row_is_shared
shared by tool-use (hoe/water/harvest) AND sword swing -- see action_row_is_shared

direction_order_note: Stardew order: Down, Right, Up, Left. This is NOT the same order as the old ad-hoc layout (which was Down, Up, Left, Right) -- do not assume row index by memory, always read row_map above.
action_row_is_shared: Rows 4-7 are a single 4-frame 'action' animation per direction used for BOTH farm-tool use (hoe till, watering can, harvest) AND the sword swing -- there is no separate tool-vs-weapon row. The 4 frames read as windup, swing/strike, follow-through, recover. Slicer builds one 'action_<dir>' SpriteFrames animation per direction from these rows; consumers map both use-tool and sword-swing states onto action_<dir>.
left_is_drawn_not_mirrored: The LEFT row (row 3 for walk, row 7 for action) is its OWN authored artwork, matching the existing distinct left/right side profiles already in this project (a leading-edge nose/chin bump makes left vs right read as facing, not a mirror). The slicer must NOT flip_h the right row to derive left, and must NOT flip_h the left row to derive right -- both are read directly off their own row, as drawn.
idle_note: There is no separate 'idle' row. idle_<dir> is a 1-frame animation built from column 0 of that direction's walk_<dir> row (the same idle/standing pose the walker returns to). This keeps the sheet at exactly 8 rows while still exposing idle_down/idle_right/idle_up/idle_left as named animations, matching the pre-existing animation-name contract consumers call.
single_frame_fallback: char_<id>.png (single 16x32 PNG, down-facing idle pose) is KEPT as a non-animated fallback -- unchanged in size/purpose from before this stride. SpriteSheets.build_character() falls back to it (via PlaceholderFrames) when a sheet is missing or is not exactly 64x256.

enemy_sheets.note: ENEMIES ARE NOT FARMER-FORMAT. This is a completely separate, simpler, single-row layout, unchanged by this stride. Only the 9 CHARACTERS (player + 8 NPCs: marta, sten, bram, rosa, alden, finn, willow, garrick) use the 8-row farmer format above -- slime/barn_slime/wisp/goblin/slime_king all keep this format.
enemy_sheets.frame_note: char_<id>_sheet.png for enemies is a single row: idle1, idle2 (bob), hurt (flash/knockback pose), die (squash/fade pose). Frame size matches the enemy's own char_<id>.png size (16x16 slime/wisp, 16x24 goblin, 48x48 slime_king).
